channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Vance-Walz VP debate
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) met face-to-face in New York City on Tuesday in the first and likely only vice presidential debate of the election cycle, a high-stakes moment that comes a little more than a month before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: “This strike is about fairness. Foreign-owned shipping companies have made record profits and executive compensation has grown,” Harris said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Trio of crises looms over the campaign's final stretch
item.description: In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance went after each other’s running mates Tuesday and sought to shore up their campaigns’ vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Several questions about Walz's record not asked about during vice presidential debate
item.description: Several controversial moments from Gov. Tim Walz's past were not mentioned during the first and only vice presidential debate on Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 5 clashes between Vance and Walz during debate showdown: 'Your mics are cut'
item.description: Fox News Digital compiled the top five clashes between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz during their first and only vice presidential debate this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative social media reactions pour in declaring winner of VP debate: 'This was a massacre'
item.description: Social media reactions from conservatives came pouring in on social media following the first and only vice presidential debate on Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, Matt Sepic, and Nicole Ki, MPR News

item.title: Best and worst debate moments for Vance, Walz
item.description: Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) engaged in a policy-focused debate Tuesday, civilly sparring on immigration, abortion and the economy, among other issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he would veto national abortion ban
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday said he would veto a federal abortion ban if it made it to his desk in a potential second term after previously refusing to say whether he would do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Austrian far-right party wins national vote but its chances of governing are unclear
item.description: The Freedom Party on Sunday secured the first far-right national parliamentary election victory in post-World War II Austria. But political rivals have said they won’t work with the party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Pentagon says it wants to prevent Iran from spreading the conflict in the Middle East
item.description: After Israel killed Hezbollah’s leader, the U.S. says it wants to stop Iran from “exploiting the situation or expanding the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Kamala Harris presents case for rebuilding the middle class in Las Vegas campaign stop
item.description: Kamala Harris wrapped up her second visit to Las Vegas since she became the Democratic presidential candidate, focusing on multiple policy issues.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PIX Panel discusses historic indictment against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – History was made in New York City this week when Eric Adams became the city’s first sitting mayor to be indicted on federal charges. A federal indictment charged Adams with bribery, campaign finance and conspiracy offenses.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump tops Harris by 6 points in Arizona: Survey
item.description: The new USA TODAY/Suffolk University survey found Trump leads the vice president, 48 percent to 42 percent, in the Grand Canyon State.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
